title: Agricultural Scientist / Agronomist
description: Improve crop yields, soil health, and farming practices through research and field trials. Advise farmers on best-practice agronomy, pest management, irrigation, and sustainable land use.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — food security drive and agri-extension services need agronomists.
employability.outlook: Growing; precision agriculture and climate adaptation increasing demand.
pathways: DALRRD, ARC, agro-chemical companies, commercial farms, cooperatives

title: Veterinarian
description: Diagnose and treat diseases in animals — companion animals, livestock, and wildlife. Vets work in private practice, public health, the agriculture sector, and conservation.

employability.demand: High — shortage of vets in state veterinary services and rural practices.
employability.outlook: Strong; biosecurity, food safety regulation, and wildlife conservation driving demand.
pathways: Private practice, DALRRD, wildlife organisations, game farms

title: Animal Scientist
description: Study animal nutrition, genetics, breeding, and welfare to improve livestock productivity and health. Work with beef, dairy, poultry, and small-stock operations and research institutions.

employability.demand: Moderate — livestock sector and feed industry are main employers.
employability.outlook: Stable; sustainability and alternative proteins creating new research niches.
pathways: Commercial livestock farms, ARC, feed companies, DALRRD

title: Farm / Agribusiness Manager
description: Plan and manage agricultural operations — crop planning, resource allocation, staff management, sales, and compliance. Agribusiness managers handle commercial farming enterprises or agri-processing companies.

employability.demand: High — commercial farming sector constantly needs capable managers.
employability.outlook: Strong; agriprocessing, export markets, and land reform creating new businesses.
pathways: BSc Agri Management / BCom Agribusiness (4 years)

title: Conservation Scientist / Ecologist
description: Protect and restore natural ecosystems — conducting biodiversity assessments, developing conservation plans, and advising on land use. Work in national parks, NGOs, and consulting.

employability.demand: Moderate — driven by biodiversity legislation and conservation funding.
employability.outlook: Steady; international conservation funding supplements government posts.

title: Food Technologist
description: Develop, improve, and ensure the safety of food products — from formulation and processing to quality control and regulatory compliance. Bridges science and the food industry.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — food industry is one of SA's largest manufacturing sectors.
employability.outlook: Stable; health foods, export compliance, and functional foods growing.
pathways: BSc Food Science / Food Technology (4 years)
ARC, food and beverage manufacturers, retailers, government labs

title: Environmental Manager / EIA Practitioner
description: Oversee environmental compliance, conduct environmental impact assessments, and develop sustainability plans. Required on all major infrastructure, mining, and development projects by law.

employability.demand: High — NEMA requires EAPS registration for all EIA projects.
employability.outlook: Growing; regulatory tightening and ESG reporting driving corporate demand.
pathways: Consulting firms, mining companies, government, development agencies

title: Hydrologist / Water Resources Specialist
description: Study the movement and distribution of water — rivers, groundwater, wetlands, and water supply systems. Support dam design, drought management, and water quality work.

employability.demand: High — water scarcity is SA's most pressing resource challenge.
employability.outlook: Strong; demand growing with infrastructure investment in water security.
pathways: BSc Hydrology / Geography / Civil Engineering (3–4 years)
DWS, consulting firms, municipalities, catchment management agencies
